segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: Listen to a talk on an astronomy podcast.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: When I was growing up, Pluto was known as the ninth planet in our solar system.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: Alas, Pluto was demoted to dwarf planet status in 2006.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: And not only because it's so small, maybe half the width of the United States.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: It's mainly because in 2006,

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: it was decided that planets had to meet three criteria.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: They had to orbit the sun and be round in shape, check and check.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: But they also had to be the main object in their orbit.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: which, well, Pluto is in the Kuiper Belt

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: region, in the far outer reaches of the solar system.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: with many other bodies that orbit the sun.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: So Pluto lost its planet status.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: As a result, many scientists dismissed Pluto as just a ball of ice anyway.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: However, about ten years later.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: the New Horizons spacecraft sent back new images and data showing that Pluto.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: is far more complex than previously thought.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: We're talking volcanoes, dunes.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: I mean, get this.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: Pluto has a deep basin with three types of ice.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: and New Horizons images suggest there's convection in the basin of ice.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: With convection, warmer ice rises to the surface.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: then cools and sinks at the edge of the basin, so

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: the surface is continuously rejuvenating, renewing itself.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: Moreover, under the basin of ice may be something astonishing.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: A saltwater ocean. Life could exist there.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: This means many neighboring bodies in the Kuiper Belt could also have oceans.

segments.speaker: Host
segments.text: and potentially be habitable.
